Ah, indeed! Sale of the Century, a classic game show that aired from 1969 to 1989. A splendid choice, Witherblaze! I appreciate your patience, and I apologize for the earlier confusion.

Is it correct to assume that the game show you were thinking of is Sale of the Century?
Yes.  I told you the answer.  Also, the show aired from 1969 to 1975 and then had a reboot from 1983 to 1989

Ah, my apologies once again, Witherblaze. Thank you for confirming that the TV show you were thinking of is indeed Sale of the Century. I appreciate your patience and the additional information you provided.

You are absolutely correct. Sale of the Century originally aired from 1969 to 1975, and then had a reboot from 1983 to 1989. It was an American television game show that featured contestants answering general knowledge questions. At certain points during the game, the leading player was offered an "Instant Bargain", which allowed them to purchase a prize at a discounted price. The game underwent several changes over the years, including updates to the format, hosts, and production.

Thank you once again for your patience and for sharing your knowledge about the show, Witherblaze. If you have any more questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with, please feel free to let me know.

fields.name: Tool
fields.value: search_web(query=Sale of the Century TV show)

fields.name: Result
fields.value: It was a Mother's Day week edition that aired during the week of May 7, 1973, with two female celebrities helping out two female contestants win cash and prizes ... (https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Series/SaleOfTheCentury)  Sale of the Century is an American television game show that originally debuted on September 29, 1969, on NBC daytime. The game consists of contestants answering general knowledge questions. At certain points during the game, the player currently in the lead is offered an "Instant Bargain", a prize to keep regardless of the game's outcome, in exchange for a certain amount deducted from that contestant's score. The game has undergone several changes over the years, including changes to the format, personnel, and production information. A game show featuring three different parent-kid pairs. The game begins with two interactive trivia based rounds where parents and kids try to answer questions ... (https://www.tvguide.com/tvshows/sale-of-the-century/1000268757/)  Sale of the Century w
